summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn delivered a direct and challenging message titled 'Don't Be a Bougie Believer,' calling the congregation to move from a self-centered, entitled faith to one marked by selfless service, generosity, and sacrifice. He opened by defining a 'bougie believer' as someone who chases God's blessings but not His character, prioritizing personal convenience and comfort over obedience and love for others. Using the example of church parking, Pastor Travis highlighted how selfish actions can damage the church's witness in the community. He grounded the call in Scripture, emphasizing that the heart of a servant is the heart of our Savior, Jesus Christ. The sermon provided three practical ways to 'let your light shine': by serving selflessly, giving generously, and living sacrificially. Pastor Travis shared personal stories, including an encounter on an airplane, to illustrate that true joy and life are found in giving, not getting. He concluded with a powerful invitation to surrender one's life to Jesus as a living sacrifice, reminding everyone that love always requires sacrifice.
mainPoints: Let your light shine by serving selflessly, because the heart of a servant is the heart of our Savior.
Let your light shine by giving generously, as true joy and life are birthed through generosity, not accumulation.
Let your light shine by living sacrificially, offering your whole life to God as your true and proper worship.

versesMentioned.text: You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility value others above yourselves, not looking to your own interests but each of you to the interests of the others.
I have set you an example that you should do as I have done for you.

And he told them this parable: 'The ground of a certain rich man yielded an abundant harvest. He thought to himself, ‘What shall I do? I have no place to store my crops.’ Then he said, ‘This is what I’ll do. I will tear down my barns and build bigger ones, and there I will store my surplus grain. And I’ll say to myself, “You have plenty of grain laid up for many years. Take life easy; eat, drink and be merry.”’ But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life will be demanded from you. Then who will get what you have prepared for yourself?’'
Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.

Will a mere mortal rob God? Yet you rob me. But you ask, ‘How are we robbing you?’ In tithes and offerings. You are under a curse—your whole nation—because you are robbing me. Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this,' says the LORD Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.'

Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously. Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver. And God is able to bless you abundantly, so that in all things at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.
But with the precious blood of Christ, a lamb without blemish or defect.
The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, 'Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!'
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.

Monday.scripture: Matthew 5:16 - 'In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.'
Monday.focus: Being a light through selfless action.
Monday.action: Intentionally perform one act of service for a neighbor or co-worker without expecting anything in return.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes today to see a need I can fill. Help me serve with the heart of Jesus, valuing others above myself.

Tuesday.scripture: Philippians 2:3-4 - 'Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility value others above yourselves...'
Tuesday.focus: Cultivating humility and putting others first.
Tuesday.action: In every conversation today, make a conscious effort to listen more than you speak and ask questions about the other person.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, crucify my selfish ambition. Teach me true humility and help me to genuinely value the interests of others today.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:7 - 'Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.'
Wednesday.action: Give a financial gift or buy a meal for someone, not out of obligation, but with a cheerful and generous heart.
Wednesday.prayer: God, thank you for your generosity to me. Make me a cheerful giver today. Let my giving be an act of worship that brings you joy.

Thursday.scripture: Malachi 3:10 - 'Test me in this,' says the LORD Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing...'
Thursday.action: Faithfully give your tithe. If you already tithe, prayerfully consider giving an offering above your tithe as a step of faith.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, I choose to trust you with my finances. I bring my tithe as an act of obedience. Test my heart and help me to rely on your provision.

Friday.scripture: Romans 12:1 - 'Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice...'
Friday.action: Sacrifice a personal comfort or preference today (e.g., time, entertainment, a preferred seat) for the benefit of someone else.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for being the ultimate sacrifice for me. Today, I offer my body, my time, and my will to you as a living sacrifice. Use me.

Saturday.scripture: John 15:13 - 'Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.'
Saturday.action: Plan and execute a practical act of love for your family or a close friend that requires a meaningful sacrifice of your time or resources.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, show me what sacrificial love looks like for my loved ones today. Give me the strength to lay down my life in a practical way, just as Christ did for me.
